The source appears to be a small Electron/Vite TypeScript app. I found the current project metadata only carries a boolean `isGitRepository`, and the visible project header lives in `src/renderer/src/App.tsx`; next I’m reading those source files and tests to fit the change into the existing IPC/data flow.

{
  "name": "relay",

    "dev": "electron-vite dev",

    "logs": "mkdir -p \"$HOME/Library/Application Support/relay\" && touch \"$HOME/Library/Application Support/relay/relay.log\" && tail -n 200 -f \"$HOME/Library/Application Support/relay/relay.log\"",
    "logs:dev": "touch /tmp/relay-dev.log && tail -n 200 -f /tmp/relay-dev.log",
    "preview": "electron-vite preview",
    "test": "node tests/run-tests.mjs",
    "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@dnd-kit/core": "^6.3.1",

    "zod": "^3.24.1"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@types/node": "^22.10.2",

    "electron": "^33.2.1",
    "electron-vite": "^2.3.0",
    "typescript": "^5.7.2",
    "vite": "^5.4.11"
  }
}

Create Ticket

Draft a clear implementation ticket from a rough idea.

I’m going to make Git status a separate async IPC path. That keeps the existing project list and board reads fast, while the renderer can show a compact loading pill and update it when the project list or active project refreshes.
